evals.prompt: Perform a STRIDE threat model for our NestJS payment service. It handles credit card processing via Stripe, stores payment records in PostgreSQL, and is called by the API gateway. Users authenticate via JWT. The service also sends payment confirmation emails via SendGrid.
evals.expected_output: STRIDE analysis with attack surface discovery, per-component threat table, severity ratings, mitigations, and data flow diagram

assertions.name: attack_surface_discovery
assertions.description: Discovers auth boundaries, data ingress points, storage, and external services

assertions.name: stride_coverage
assertions.description: All 6 STRIDE categories evaluated for the payment flow

assertions.name: severity_ratings
assertions.description: Each finding has Critical/High/Medium/Low severity

assertions.name: specific_threats
assertions.description: Threats reference specific components (Stripe API, JWT, PostgreSQL, SendGrid)

assertions.name: mitigations_proposed
assertions.description: Mitigations provided for High and Critical findings

assertions.name: data_flow_diagram
assertions.description: Shows trust boundaries between gateway, payment service, Stripe, and DB

evals.prompt: We're adding OAuth2 login (Google, GitHub) to our React + NestJS app. Threat model the authentication flow — from the user clicking 'Login with Google' to receiving a JWT. Focus on token handling, callback vulnerabilities, and session management.
evals.expected_output: Focused threat model on OAuth2 flow with specific attacks (CSRF on callback, token leakage, open redirect) and mitigations

assertions.name: oauth_specific_threats
assertions.description: Identifies OAuth-specific attacks: CSRF on callback, open redirect, token leakage

assertions.name: token_handling
assertions.description: Analyzes how access/refresh tokens are stored and transmitted

assertions.name: callback_security
assertions.description: Reviews callback URL validation and state parameter usage

assertions.name: severity_ratings
assertions.description: Each threat has a severity rating

assertions.name: mitigations
assertions.description: Specific mitigations for each identified threat

evals.prompt: Threat model our file upload feature. Users upload images and PDFs (max 50MB) to a NestJS endpoint, files are stored in S3, and a Lambda processes them (resize images, extract PDF text). Results are stored in DynamoDB.
evals.expected_output: Threat model covering upload validation, S3 access controls, Lambda execution risks, and data flow between services

assertions.name: upload_threats
assertions.description: Identifies malicious file upload risks (malware, path traversal, content-type spoofing)

assertions.name: s3_access_threats
assertions.description: Reviews S3 bucket permissions, presigned URL risks, and public access

assertions.name: lambda_risks
assertions.description: Considers Lambda execution environment risks (code injection, resource exhaustion)

assertions.name: severity_ratings
assertions.description: All findings severity-rated

assertions.name: trust_boundaries
assertions.description: Shows trust boundaries between user, API, S3, Lambda, and DynamoDB

evals.prompt: We're building a multi-tenant SaaS platform. Tenants share the same database (row-level isolation via tenant_id). Perform STRIDE threat modeling focused on tenant isolation — can one tenant access another's data?
evals.expected_output: Focused threat model on tenant isolation attacks with specific SQL injection, IDOR, and side-channel vectors

assertions.name: isolation_threats
assertions.description: Identifies cross-tenant data access vectors (missing WHERE tenant_id, IDOR)

assertions.name: sql_injection_risk
assertions.description: Evaluates SQL injection risk for bypassing tenant_id filters

assertions.name: api_level_isolation
assertions.description: Checks if every API endpoint enforces tenant_id from JWT, not from request body

assertions.name: severity_ratings
assertions.description: Isolation breaches rated as Critical

assertions.name: mitigations
assertions.description: Row-level security policies, middleware enforcement, and testing recommendations

evals.prompt: Perform a STRIDE analysis on our WebSocket-based chat application. Users connect via Socket.IO, messages are broadcast to room members, and message history is stored in MongoDB. Users authenticate once on connection with a JWT.
evals.expected_output: STRIDE analysis covering WebSocket-specific threats: connection hijacking, message injection, DoS via connection flood, and auth token handling

assertions.name: websocket_specific
assertions.description: Identifies WebSocket-specific threats (connection hijacking, message replay, origin validation)

assertions.name: auth_on_connection
assertions.description: Reviews JWT validation on WebSocket handshake and reconnection

assertions.name: message_injection
assertions.description: Considers XSS via injected message content

assertions.name: dos_vectors
assertions.description: Identifies DoS via connection flooding or large message payloads

assertions.name: severity_ratings
assertions.description: All findings severity-rated

assertions.name: room_authorization
assertions.description: Reviews whether users can only join rooms they're authorized for
